label: Launch
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Launch from Kennedy Space Center with Orion and Lunar View co-manifested.

label: Low-Earth orbit checks
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Mission control and crew complete final checks in low Earth orbit.

label: Translunar injection
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: The upper stage sends Orion and Lunar View toward the Moon.

label: Transposition and extraction
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Orion separates, turns around, docks with Lunar View, and extracts it from the upper stage.

label: Outbound cruise
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Orion and Lunar View coast toward the Moon.

label: Lunar flyby / gravity assist
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: The detailed ESA public profile includes a lunar flyby / gravity-assist step before Gateway arrival.

label: NRHO arrival
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Arrival at Gateway's near-rectilinear halo orbit.

label: Dock with Gateway
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Orion docks with Gateway and connects / installs Lunar View.

label: Transfer and activation
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Crew and cargo transfer to Gateway, Lunar View is activated, and the lander is prepared.

label: Surface departure
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Two astronauts undock in the lander for a South Pole surface sortie.

label: Surface / orbital split operations
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Two astronauts work on the surface while Orion remains docked to Gateway for about six days in NRHO.

label: Surface ascent and redock
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: The lander lifts off from the Moon and redocks with Gateway.

label: Prepare for Earth return
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Crew and samples transfer back for the Earth-return leg.

label: Departure from NRHO
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Orion departs Gateway's NRHO at the optimal Earth-return point.

label: Return flyby and cruise
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: The detailed public profile includes a lunar gravity-assist burn and homeward cruise.

label: Reentry and splashdown
sourceNote: Archived/provider-specific Artemis V phase reconstruction from older NASA procurement and ESA detail sources; timings are notional.
description: Crew module separates, reenters Earth's atmosphere, and splashes down in the Pacific.
